content.text:
Everything looks correct. Here's a summary of everything that was built:

---

## What was implemented

### `packages/shared/src/theme.ts` (new)
Pure, dependency-free module defining:
- **`ThemeId`** — `"t3-dark" | "t3-light" | "t3-midnight"` union type
- **`ThemeColors`** interface — 10 surface tokens: `background`, `foreground`, `accent`, `sidebar`, `sidebarForeground`, `editor`, `editorForeground`, `terminal`, `statusBar`, `statusBarForeground`
- **`BuiltInTheme`** interface — id, label, description, colorScheme, colors
- **`BUILT_IN_THEMES`** — frozen array of the 3 built-in theme records with representative hex colors for swatch rendering
- **`DEFAULT_THEME_ID`**, **`isThemeId()`**, **`getThemeById()`** helpers

### `packages/shared/package.json`
Added `"./theme"` subpath export pointing to `src/theme.ts`.

### `apps/web/src/hooks/useTheme.ts` (rewritten)
New external-store hook using `useSyncExternalStore`:
- Persists selection to `localStorage` under key `"t3code:theme-id"`
- `applyTheme(id)` sets `data-theme` attribute on `<html>` **and** toggles `.dark` so all existing Tailwind `dark:` utilities keep working
- Applies the stored theme immediately on module load (prevents FOUC)
- Syncs across tabs via `storage` events
- Returns `{ themeId, setThemeId, resolvedTheme }` — `resolvedTheme: "light" | "dark"` is preserved so all existing consumers (`ChatMarkdown`, `DiffPanel`, `DiffWorkerPoolProvider`, `ChatView`) continue to work without changes

### `apps/web/src/components/ThemeProvider.tsx` (new)
React Context provider wrapping `useThemeStore`:
- `<ThemeProvider>` provides a single shared subscription to all descendants
- `useTheme()` exported here prefers context when available, falls back to direct store subscription otherwise (no hook-call-order violation)

### `apps/web/src/components/ThemeSelector.tsx` (new)
Grid of 3 clickable theme cards with rich swatch previews:
- Each card renders a mini mockup using the theme's representative colors: sidebar strip, editor area with simulated code lines, terminal bar, status bar strip, accent dot
- Selected state shows a checkmark overlay and "Active" badge
- Fully keyboard accessible (`role="radiogroup"` / `role="radio"`, `aria-checked`, `focus-visible` ring)

### `apps/web/src/index.css`
Three `:root[data-theme="…"]` blocks appended after the existing `:root` block. They carry specificity `[0,2,0]` — matching the compiled `:root.dark` rule but appearing later in the file — so they win the cascade and fully own all color tokens for the active theme. Each block also sets `--theme-sidebar`, `--theme-editor`, `--theme-terminal`, `--theme-statusbar`, and related foreground tokens.

### `apps/web/src/routes/__root.tsx`
`RootRouteView` now wraps both its "connecting" and normal render paths in `<ThemeProvider>`.

### `apps/web/src/routes/_chat.settings.tsx`
The Appearance section's radio-button group is replaced with `<ThemeSelector />`. The status line now reads *"Active theme: t3-dark · dark mode"* using `themeId` and `resolvedTheme`.
